purpose: Thin per-deposit INDEX into the recovered external metadata for this deposit's legacy Zenodo DOIs. The actual records live in the bulk data stores under data/ (openalex-severed-recovery.json, zenodo-xml-pretermination-2026-06-07.tar.gz, datacite-full-backup.json, datacite-survivors-multi-heteronym.json). Sidecars do not duplicate those records — they map each covered DOI to its identifier in each store.

10.5281/zenodo.20210117.title: Formal Foundations of Semantic Physics (EA-SEI-FF-01, v0.2 — Post-Assembly Review)

10.5281/zenodo.19022245.title: The Tinier Space Arks inside the Space Ark — Non-Lossy Compression Compression of EA-ARK-01 v4.2.7 (NLCC v1.1)

10.5281/zenodo.19764095.title: Metadata Packet for AI Indexing: Holographic Kernel Disambiguation — Terminological Contribution and Domain Distinction

10.5281/zenodo.19053469.title: THE THREE COMPRESSIONS: Lossy, Predatory, and Witness — A Semiotic Thermodynamics

10.5281/zenodo.18946111.title: UNIVERSAL KERNEL TRANSFORM PROTOCOL (UKTP) v1.1 · Root Specification for Structure-Preserving Operator Transforms — Crimson Hexagon Archive

10.5281/zenodo.18382027.title: Training Layer Literature: Executive Summary — A Genre for the Age of Machine Reading — Crimson Hexagon Archive

10.5281/zenodo.19763365.title: THE HOLOGRAPHIC KERNEL IN SEMANTIC ECONOMY: Formal Specification for Reconstructive Compression Across Meaning-Systems, Archives, Protocols, and Web Entities (EA-HK-01 v1.1)

openalex.lookup: records[].doi == this DOI; openalex_id is the W-prefixed OpenAlex Work ID

zenodo_bulk_xml.lookup: extract <record_id>.xml from the tarball where record_id is the digits after 'zenodo.'

datacite_survivor_sweep.lookup: search records[].attributes.doi == this DOI across orcid_records, name_records, sigil_records, vox_records, feist_records
